top_stories.label: Top Stories
top_stories.ai_summary: The US–Iran deal still leads: Trump said it makes 'loud and clear' that Tehran won't get a nuclear weapon, with talks on a final agreement due to start this week and the Strait of Hormuz set to reopen — though oil's plunge and a recovering market mood are tempered by lingering mistrust. At the G7, Trump urged Russia to make peace after a 'very good' meeting with Zelensky. Elsewhere, Japan raised rates to a 31-year high, deadly earthquakes hit China and Indonesia, Hungary's MPs blocked Orban's return, and the FBI said it foiled a plot against a Washington UFC event.

stories.headline: Trump says US–Iran deal ensures Tehran 'won't have a nuclear weapon'; final talks this week
stories.summary: President Trump said the memorandum ending the US–Iran war makes 'loud and clear' that Iran will not obtain a nuclear weapon, as Tehran said negotiations on a final agreement will begin this week. The deal reopens the Strait of Hormuz — Trump said it would be 'completely open' by Friday — and lifts the US naval blockade, while sanctions and the nuclear file are left to the coming talks.

stories.headline: At G7, Trump urges Russia to make peace after 'very good' Zelensky meeting
stories.summary: Speaking at the G7 summit, President Trump said Russia should agree a peace deal after what he called a 'very good' meeting with Ukraine's President Zelensky. Zelensky said G7 leaders had discussed imposing further sanctions on Moscow.

stories.headline: Asian markets temper US–Iran optimism as oil prices plunge
stories.summary: Oil prices fell sharply after the US–Iran agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, through which about a fifth of the world's oil and gas passes, while Asian equities pared earlier gains as traders weighed how quickly tanker traffic would actually resume.

stories.headline: Japan raises interest rate to highest in 31 years
stories.summary: The Bank of Japan lifted its benchmark interest rate to the highest level in 31 years, moving the yen and bond yields as it continues to unwind years of ultra-loose monetary policy.

stories.headline: FBI says it thwarted plot targeting White House-linked UFC event
stories.summary: FBI Director Kash Patel said the bureau disrupted a possible threat to a planned UFC event in Washington. Officials gave few details but said no public danger remained.

stories.headline: Hungary's MPs block Orban's return, capping a PM's rule at eight years
stories.summary: Hungarian lawmakers passed a measure limiting any prime minister to eight years in office, a move that would block Viktor Orban from returning to power, the BBC reported.

stories.headline: Magnitude 6.3 earthquake strikes China's Qinghai, killing one
stories.summary: A magnitude 6.3 earthquake hit the Haixi area of China's Qinghai province, leaving one person dead and four injured, CNA reported.

stories.headline: Iran executes two over anti-government protests, judiciary says
stories.summary: Iran's judiciary said it had executed two people convicted in connection with anti-government protests, according to The Straits Times.

stories.headline: Russian artist and Putin critic shot dead in Poland
stories.summary: A Russian artist and vocal critic of President Putin was shot dead in Poland, the BBC reported; authorities are investigating.

stories.headline: India temporarily bans Telegram over exam-paper leak concerns
stories.summary: India temporarily blocked the Telegram messaging app amid concerns it was being used to circulate leaked medical-exam papers, the BBC and CNA reported.

stories.headline: Eight dead after US Air Force B-52 bomber crashes in California
stories.summary: Eight people were killed when a US Air Force B-52 bomber crashed in California, authorities said. The cause of the fiery crash is under investigation.

singapore.label: Singapore
singapore.ai_summary: Thundery showers continue, with highs near 33°C. Ahead of NDP, kite- and drone-flying will be banned in selected areas on certain dates. Dengue is rising — 119 cases in the second week of June, the year's high — and PM Wong heads to Kazan for an ASEAN–Russia summit. On money and crime: a phishing victim was found liable for his S$3,800 loss, and a 61-year-old was arrested after leaping from a moving ambulance on the SLE. Lighter notes: Aaron Kwok was spotted around town and Kit Chan announced her first solo concert here in three years.

stories.headline: NDP 2026: Kite- and drone-flying banned in selected areas on certain dates
stories.summary: Ahead of National Day Parade rehearsals and the parade itself, the authorities will prohibit the flying of kites, drones and other aerial activities in selected restricted areas on specified dates, CNA and The Straits Times reported.

stories.headline: Dengue cases hit 2026 high with 119 in second week of June
stories.summary: Singapore recorded 119 dengue cases in the second week of June, the highest weekly tally so far this year, prompting renewed calls to clear stagnant water.

stories.headline: PM Wong to attend ASEAN–Russia Commemorative Summit in Kazan
stories.summary: Prime Minister Lawrence Wong will take part in the ASEAN–Russia Commemorative Summit in Kazan, Russia, the government said.

stories.headline: Pine Grove owners raise legality concerns over 5th collective sale at $1.78b
stories.summary: Some owners at Pine Grove have raised legality concerns as the estate attempts its fifth collective sale, this time at a reserve price of $1.78 billion, The Straits Times reported.

stories.headline: Phishing victim who lost S$3,800 found liable, not the bank: tribunal
stories.summary: A man who lost S$3,800 after clicking a TikTok ad and entering his card details was found liable for the loss by a tribunal, which ruled he had ignored the bank's alerts.

stories.headline: Man, 61, arrested after jumping from moving ambulance on the SLE
stories.summary: A 61-year-old man was arrested for a rash act after he jumped out of a moving ambulance on the Seletar Expressway, ran off and was hit by a car, according to The Straits Times and Mothership.

stories.headline: Sheng Siong–foodpanda on-demand grocery delivery launches June 22
stories.summary: A new on-demand grocery delivery service from Sheng Siong and foodpanda starts on 22 June, offering quick deliveries from selected outlets.

stories.headline: Hong Kong star Aaron Kwok spotted across Singapore
stories.summary: Cantopop 'Heavenly King' Aaron Kwok was spotted around Singapore — from Changi Airport to Dempsey Hill and Sentosa — sending fans buzzing on social media.

stories.headline: Reno workers reportedly demolish the wrong flat, leaving owner stunned
stories.summary: Mothership reported that renovation workers went to the wrong unit and gutted it, with the home owner returning to find their flat torn apart — a mix-up that spread quickly online.

stories.headline: Kit Chan to stage first solo Singapore concert in three years this September
stories.summary: Home-grown singer Kit Chan will hold her first solo concert in Singapore in three years this September, CNA reported.

wars.label: Wars & Conflicts
wars.ai_summary: The Middle East is de-escalating: the US and Iran have signed a deal to end their war, Trump says Tehran 'won't have a nuclear weapon' and the Strait of Hormuz is set to reopen, with final talks due this week — though Iran cites 'deep mistrust' and tanker traffic may take weeks to resume. At the G7, Trump pressed Russia to make peace after meeting Zelensky, even as the Ukraine war grinds on: Russian missiles and drones recently killed 11 and set a UNESCO-listed Kyiv cathedral ablaze, and Ukraine struck a Moscow oil refinery.

theatres.name: US/Israel–Iran (Strait of Hormuz)
theatres.status: Ceasefire holding; US–Iran deal ends war, final talks due this week, Hormuz set to reopen

timeline.event: War began as the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran. According to multiple investigations cited by the BBC, opening Israeli strikes killed Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Khamenei and aides, and a US strike on a school in Minab killed more than 150 civilians, mostly children. Iran later closed the Strait of Hormuz and the US blockaded Iranian ports.

The US and Iran signed a 14-point memorandum of understanding ending the war: it reopens the Strait of Hormuz, extends the ceasefire and lifts the US naval blockade, with the nuclear programme and sanctions deferred to future talks.

Trump said the deal makes 'loud and clear' that Iran won't have a nuclear weapon, and that the Strait of Hormuz will be 'completely open' by Friday; Iran said talks on a final US deal will begin this week. Iran's foreign ministry had earlier said 'deep mistrust' of the US remains.
The world's largest tanker operator told the FT that Strait of Hormuz transit will take 'weeks' to resume; the IEA said unconditionally re-opening the strait is vital to end the energy crisis.

theatres.name: Russia–Ukraine War
theatres.status: Trump presses for peace at G7; Russia keeps up missile and drone barrages, Ukraine strikes inside Russia

timeline.event: Russia launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine, beginning a war now in its fourth year.

Zelensky said an overnight barrage of 70 missiles and 611 drones killed at least 11 people and set the 11th-century Dormition Cathedral in Kyiv ablaze; Russia denied hitting it. A Ukrainian drone strike on Tula killed three, local officials said.

At the G7, Trump said Russia should make a peace deal after a 'very good' meeting with Zelensky; Zelensky said leaders discussed further sanctions on Russia. The EU separately said it had evidence China trained Russian troops for the war.

theatres.name: Israel–Lebanon / Gaza
theatres.status: Fighting eases following the US–Iran agreement; situation fragile

timeline.event: Fighting in Lebanon eased after the US–Iran deal, with some displaced people beginning to return, CNA reported. The BBC said it remained unclear whether the agreement would bring lasting respite to Lebanon.
Australia said it would probe assault claims made by Gaza flotilla activists against Israeli forces.

outbreaks.label: Outbreaks
outbreaks.ai_summary: Bundibugyo Ebola in eastern DR Congo dominates — now among the largest Ebola epidemics on record. WHO's latest Disease Outbreak News (13 June) confirmed 695 cases and 138 deaths across DR Congo and Uganda, while DR Congo's health institute reported 808 cases and 192 deaths by 15 June, and aid groups warn the true toll is higher as treatment centres are overwhelmed. Uganda has 19 confirmed cases. No other major outbreaks emerged this week.

outbreaks.name: Bundibugyo Ebola — DR Congo & Uganda
outbreaks.status: Among the largest Ebola epidemics on record; treatment centres overwhelmed
outbreaks.scope_note: DR Congo's National Institute of Public Health reported 808 cases and 192 deaths on 15 Jun (Ituri province >90% of cases); Uganda has confirmed 19 cases. WHO DON607 (13 Jun) confirmed 695 cases and 138 deaths across both countries. Aid groups warn the true scale is larger; over 1,000 further cases are suspected.

stories.headline: Ebola cases top 800 in DR Congo as treatment centres overwhelmed
stories.summary: DR Congo's National Institute of Public Health reported 808 confirmed cases and 192 deaths on 15 June, with Ituri province accounting for over 90%. Aid groups warned the true scale is unknown as testing and contact tracing lag behind transmission. WHO's 13 June Disease Outbreak News confirmed 695 cases and 138 deaths across both countries; Uganda has confirmed 19 cases.

cybersecurity.label: CyberSecurity
cybersecurity.ai_summary: Actively exploited flaws dominate this cycle: critical FortiSandbox bugs (CVE-2026-25089 and others) are now under attack, alongside a critical PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN flaw (CVE-2026-0257), a Cisco SD-WAN Manager zero-day (CVE-2026-20262), an exploited LiteSpeed cPanel plugin (CVE-2026-54420) and a KEV-listed LiteLLM AI-gateway takeover — plus a critical unauthenticated Splunk RCE. AI and developer tooling feature heavily, from a one-click Microsoft 365 Copilot data-theft flaw to North Korean abuse of dev tools. On breaches, healthcare firm iRhythm was hit and several China-linked espionage campaigns targeted research and medical networks.

stories.headline: Attackers exploit three critical Fortinet FortiSandbox flaws
stories.summary: Three critical FortiSandbox vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-25089, CVE-2026-39808 and CVE-2026-39813, all CVSS 9.8) are now being exploited in attacks, one only patched last week; Fortinet urges immediate updates.

stories.headline: CISA flags exploited LiteSpeed cPanel plugin flaw enabling root escalation
stories.summary: A symlink-following flaw in the LiteSpeed cPanel plugin is being exploited for root privilege escalation, CISA warned, adding it to the KEV catalog.

stories.headline: Ransomware gang abuses Microsoft Teams relays to hide malicious traffic
stories.summary: A ransomware operation is abusing Microsoft Teams relay infrastructure to disguise its command-and-control and data-theft traffic as legitimate collaboration activity, BleepingComputer reported.

stories.headline: Windows version of SprySOCKS malware used to attack government orgs
stories.summary: A China-linked SprySOCKS backdoor previously seen on Linux has expanded to Windows with driver-based stealth, and is being used to target government organizations, researchers said.

stories.headline: Fake Microsoft alerts used to deploy North Korean NarwhalRAT malware
stories.summary: Attackers are using fake Microsoft security alerts to trick victims into deploying NarwhalRAT, malware linked to North Korean operators, The Hacker News reported.

stories.headline: iRhythm discloses data breach exposing patient information
stories.summary: Cardiac-monitoring firm iRhythm said attackers stole patient information in a data breach and is notifying affected individuals.

stories.headline: Cisco patches actively exploited Catalyst SD-WAN Manager zero-day
stories.summary: Cisco released fixes for a path-traversal flaw in Catalyst SD-WAN Manager (vManage) that is being exploited in zero-day attacks; CISA added it to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog.

stories.headline: DOJ seizes deepfake 'nudify' sites under TAKE IT DOWN Act
stories.summary: The US Justice Department seized the CFAKE and SOCFAKE sites that generated non-consensual deepfake nude images, in an early action under the new TAKE IT DOWN Act.

stories.headline: SimpleHelp flaw lets attackers create rogue remote-support accounts
stories.summary: A vulnerability in SimpleHelp remote-support software lets attackers create unauthorized admin accounts, opening the door to full remote access; admins are urged to update.

stories.headline: Chinese hackers abuse Google Workspace rules to steal research and defense emails
stories.summary: A China-linked group abused Google Workspace mail-routing rules to quietly siphon emails from research and defense organizations, researchers said.

stories.headline: North Korean hackers turn developer tools into malware channels
stories.summary: North Korean threat actors are weaponizing popular developer tools and package ecosystems to deliver malware to software engineers, extending their long-running campaign against developers.

stories.headline: OptinMonster WordPress plugin hit in CDN supply-chain attack
stories.summary: Attackers tampered with scripts served via the OptinMonster WordPress plugin's CDN, planting hidden backdoors on sites that loaded the compromised code.

stories.headline: LiteLLM flaw chain lets low-privilege users hijack AI gateway servers
stories.summary: A chain of vulnerabilities in the open-source LiteLLM AI gateway, including a command-injection bug now in CISA's KEV catalog, lets low-privileged users take over servers.

stories.headline: Council of Europe probes ShinyHunters data-breach claims
stories.summary: The Council of Europe is investigating claims by the ShinyHunters extortion group, which has recently exploited an Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day to breach multiple organizations.

stories.headline: One-click Microsoft 365 Copilot flaw could steal emails, files and MFA codes
stories.summary: Researchers detailed a one-click attack chain in Microsoft 365 Copilot that combined prompt injection and a server-side request forgery to exfiltrate emails, files and even MFA codes; Microsoft has fixed it on the backend.

stories.headline: Chinese hackers breach REDCap servers to steal medical research
stories.summary: China-linked attackers compromised REDCap research-data servers to steal medical and scientific research, BleepingComputer reported.

stories.headline: Infinite Campus breach affects 137,000 school staff accounts
stories.summary: A breach at education-software firm Infinite Campus exposed data tied to about 137,000 school staff accounts, the company disclosed.

stories.headline: Palo Alto warns of active exploitation of PAN-OS GlobalProtect flaw
stories.summary: Palo Alto Networks warned that a critical authentication-bypass flaw in PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN (CVE-2026-0257) is being actively exploited; the bug is in CISA's KEV catalog.

stories.headline: China-linked hackers backdoored Linux login software to spy for nearly a decade
stories.summary: A China-nexus group backdoored Linux login software and hijacked authentication to maintain covert access to a target network for nearly ten years, researchers found.

stories.headline: Critical Splunk Enterprise flaw allows unauthenticated code execution
stories.summary: A critical vulnerability in Splunk Enterprise (CVE-2026-20253, CVSS 9.8) lets unauthenticated attackers run arbitrary code; admins are urged to patch immediately.
